text: The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the left lobe of the liver, exposing the gastro-jejunostomy site. The gastrogenostomy is then transected using robotic instruments with purple staples.

The surgeon restores gastric continuity by performing a linear staple side-to-side anastomosis between the proximal gastric pouch and the excluded stomach. An anisogastric tube is inserted through the gastric pouch into the excluded stomach to ensure patency of the anastomosis. The stapler entry hole is closed using an unabsorbable barbed suture.

The surgeon resects the omega-loop jejunum and performs a side-to-side anastomosis using staples to prevent intestinal stenosis. Following this, the mesenteric defect is closed with a running, non-absorbable suture.
The surgeon assesses anastomotic leakage by injecting methylene blue-stained saline through an orogastric tube, followed by reinforcement of the gastrogastric anastomosis with biological glue.
